segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So now I want you to discuss the lesson we've just been watching on the video and think about the ways in which origami can be a useful educational tool.
segments.zh: 现在我想让你们讨论一下刚才视频里看的课，思考一下折纸在哪些方面可以成为有用的教育工具。请和旁边的人一起讨论……

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Can you all work with the person sitting next to you ...

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I had no idea that such a simple thing like folding squares of paper to make the shape of something like a bird could be such an amazing tool.
segments.zh: 我完全没想到，像折纸这样简单的事情——把方纸折成鸟之类的形状——竟然能成为如此神奇的工具。这让我对折纸有了全新的认识。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: It's made me see origami in a whole new light.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 是啊。看到孩子们通过折纸培养各种教育技能，很有意思。视频里能看到他们真的很认真听讲，确保按正确顺序完成所有步骤来折鸟。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: It was interesting to see the educational skills the children were developing by doing origami.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: On the video you could see them really listening hard to make sure they did all the steps in the right order to make the bird.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。这节课里他们是单独操作，但看看孩子们能否在没有指导的情况下自己琢磨出折简单东西的方法，也会很有趣。那也有助于培养团队合作。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: In this lesson they were working individually but it would also be interesting to see if the children could work out how to make something simple without being given any direction.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: That would help with building teamwork as well.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Yes, but much more of a challenge.
segments.zh: 是的，但挑战性大得多。有一点让我印象特别深刻：孩子们在学习新东西的同时都玩得很开心。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: One thing that really stood out for me was that the children were all having fun while being taught something new.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Which is a key aim of any lesson with this age group.
segments.zh: 这正是这个年龄段任何课程的关键目标。虽然这些孩子折纸没问题，但对于更小的孩子，你可以用折纸来帮助练习精细运动技能。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: And although these kids had no problems with folding the paper, with younger children you could do origami to help practise fine motor skills.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Absolutely.
segments.zh: 完全同意。我们谈谈视频里看到的那些孩子吧？我把他们的名字都记下来了，还做了些笔记。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Shall we talk about the individual children we saw on the video?

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: I wrote all their names down and took some notes.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Yes, I did too.
segments.zh: 好，我也记了。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: OK, good.
segments.zh: 好的，那我们从希德开始吧。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Let's start with Sid.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: He was interesting because before they started doing the origami, he was being quite disruptive.
segments.zh: 他挺有意思的，因为在开始折纸之前，他一直很捣乱。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。他确实从动手操作中受益了——这让他安静下来，开始集中注意力。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: He really benefited from having to use his hands – it helped him to settle down and start concentrating.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Yes, I noticed that too.
segments.zh: 是的，我也注意到了。那杰克呢？我注意到他似乎想自己解决问题。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: What about Jack?

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I noticed he seemed to want to work things out for himself.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。你可以看到他在尝试不同的方法，而不是向老师求助。你觉得娜奥米怎么样？

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: You could see him trying out different things rather than asking the teacher for help.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: What did you make of Naomi?

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: She seemed to be losing interest at one point but then she decided she wanted her mouse to be the best and that motivated her to try harder.
segments.zh: 她有一阵好像失去了兴趣，但后来她决定要让自己的老鼠做得最好，这激励她更努力了。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: She didn't seem satisfied with hers in the end, though.
segments.zh: 不过最后她对自己的作品似乎并不满意。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 是的。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Anya was such a star.
segments.zh: 安雅真是个明星。她听得很仔细，然后几乎没费什么劲就折出了一只完美的鸟。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: She listened so carefully and then produced the perfect bird with very little effort.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Mmm – I think the teacher could have increased the level of difficulty for her.
segments.zh: 嗯——我觉得老师本可以给她增加点难度。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Maybe.
segments.zh: 也许吧。我想这是扎拉第一次接触折纸。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: I think it was the first time Zara had come across origami.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: She looked as if she didn't really get what was going on.
segments.zh: 她看起来好像不太明白发生了什么。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: She seemed unsure about what she was supposed to do, but in the end hers didn't turn out too badly.
segments.zh: 她似乎不确定自己该做什么，但最后她的作品也不算太差。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。我相信这对她来说是一次积极的学习经历。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I'm sure it was a positive learning experience for her.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: I think one reason why the origami activity worked so well in this class was that the teacher was well prepared.
segments.zh: 我认为折纸活动在这个班上如此成功的一个原因是老师准备充分。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。要是让我来准备示例，展示制作这只鸟的每一步，那得花很长时间。但那个主意真不错。孩子们能看到他们要达到的目标——而且能亲手拿着实物，比只看图片好多了。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I think it would have taken me ages to prepare examples, showing each of the steps involved in making the bird.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: But that was a really good idea.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: The children could see what they were aiming for – and much better for them to be able to hold something, rather than just looking at pictures.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Mmm – those physical examples supported her verbal explanations really well.
segments.zh: 嗯——那些实物示例很好地补充了她的口头讲解。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: It's strange that origami isn't used more widely.
segments.zh: 奇怪的是折纸并没有被更广泛地使用。你觉得是什么原因？

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Why do you think that is?

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Well, teachers may just feel it's not that appealing to children who are used to doing everything on computers, especially boys.
segments.zh: 嗯，老师们可能觉得折纸对那些习惯了在电脑上做所有事情的孩子——尤其是男孩——没什么吸引力。即使他们知道折纸的好处。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Even if they're aware of the benefits.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Oh, I don't know.
segments.zh: 哦，我不这么认为。它和其他手工活动没什么不同。我敢打赌是因为很多老师像我一样笨手笨脚。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: It's no different to any other craft activity.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I bet it's because so many teachers are clumsy like me.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: That's true – too much effort required if you're not good with your hands.
segments.zh: 确实——如果你手不巧，就需要付出太多努力。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: Well, anyway, I think we should try it out in our maths teaching practice with Year 3.
segments.zh: 嗯，不管怎样，我觉得我们应该在三年级数学教学实践中试试看。我能看到用折纸是一种非常吸引人的方式来巩固孩子们对几何形状的认识，就像他们在视频里做的那样，但我觉得它也很适合用来讲解分数，这个内容很快就要教了。

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: I can see using origami is a really engaging way of reinforcing children's knowledge of geometric shapes, like they were doing in the video, but I think it would also work really well for presenting fractions, which is coming up soon.

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Good idea – that's something most of the kids in that class might struggle with.
segments.zh: 好主意——这可能是那个班里大多数孩子都会觉得困难的内容。折纸也是练习对称的好方法——但我觉得他们上学期已经学过了。

segments.speaker: LIA
segments.en: Origami would also be good practice for using symmetry – but I think they did that last term.

segments.speaker: SEB
segments.en: OK – well let's try and get some ideas together and plan the lesson next week.
segments.zh: 好的，那我们试着一起想些点子，把下周的课计划一下。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: OK, if you could all stop ...
segments.zh: 好了，请大家先停一下……

segments.en: The person I've chosen to talk about is the French writer Victor Hugo – many people have heard of him because his novel, Les Misérables, which he wrote in 1862, is famous around the world.
segments.zh: 我选择介绍的人是法国作家维克多·雨果——很多人听说过他，因为他在1862年写的小说《悲惨世界》举世闻名。这部小说在20世纪80年代被改编成舞台音乐剧，2012年又推出了电影版。所以，我相信我们中有些人大概知道故事情节，但对作者本人了解得少得多。今天，我将进一步介绍这位才华横溢的人，并重点讲讲他在英吉利海峡群岛中根西岛上的家。

segments.en: It became a stage musical in the 1980s, and a film version was also released in 2012.
So, some of us, I'm sure, have a pretty general idea of the plot, but we know much less about the author.
Today, I'm going to provide a little more insight into this talented man and I'm going to talk particularly about the home he had on the island of Guernsey in the British Channel Islands.

items.prompt: Which TWO educational skills were shown in the video of children doing origami?

pairs.note: 题干说'教育技能'，原文说'被教新东西'，是概括与具体的关系。
题干'视频中展示'对应原文'让我印象深刻'，都是强调观察到的内容。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'孩子们在学习新东西时玩得很开心'，其中'being taught something new'对应题干'educational skills'，'stood out for me'对应'shown in the video'。

items.prompt: Which TWO educational skills were shown in the video of children doing origami?

pairs.note: 题干说'教育技能'，原文说'被教新东西'，是概括与具体的关系。

pairs.p: the children were all having fun while being taught something new
pairs.note: 题干提到'儿童做折纸的视频'，原文直接描述视频中的场景。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接描述了视频中儿童边玩边学新东西，对应题干问的教育技能。

pairs.note: 题干中的Sid在原文中被替换为代词He，指代同一人物。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的Sid在原文中直接以代词He出现，通过指代关系定位到答案句。

pairs.note: 题干中的Jack在原文中被替换为代词him，指代同一人。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Jack的情况，原文用him指代Jack，通过代词定位到描述Jack的句子。

pairs.note: 题干人名Naomi在原文中替换为代词She，指代同一人。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Naomi的情况，原文用She指代Naomi，直接定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位人名。

paraphrase.explain: 题干是名字Anya，原文首句出现Anya，直接定位。

pairs.note: 题干中的名字Zara在原文中被替换为代词'hers'，指代Zara的作品。

paraphrase.explain: 通过代词'hers'指代Zara，定位到原文中描述Zara作品的部分。

items.prompt: Before starting an origami activity in class, the students think it is important for the teacher to

pairs.q: Before starting an origami activity in class, the students think it is important for the teacher to
pairs.p: The children could see what they were aiming for
pairs.note: 题干问学生认为老师应该做什么，原文说孩子们能看到目标，对应老师需要展示目标。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到孩子们能看到目标，对应题干中老师需要做的事情，即展示成品。

items.prompt: The students agree that some teachers might be unwilling to use origami in class because

pairs.q: unwilling to use origami in class
pairs.note: 原文用'笨手笨脚'解释为什么老师不愿意用折纸，是因果同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接给出了原因：老师笨手笨脚，对应题干中的'不愿意用折纸'。

items.prompt: Why do the students decide to use origami in their maths teaching practice?

pairs.q: use origami in their maths teaching practice
pairs.p: using origami is a really engaging way of reinforcing children's knowledge of geometric shapes
pairs.note: 题干问为什么用折纸，原文说用折纸是一种吸引人的方式来巩固几何知识，这是原因之一。

pairs.p: I think we should try it out
pairs.note: 题干中的'decide to use'对应原文的'should try it out'，表示决定尝试。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句中学生说应该尝试用折纸，并解释原因：折纸能巩固几何知识，还能用于呈现分数。因此答案与折纸的教学价值相关。
